findings.rendered_text_content: Actual RRN structural answer rendered (NOT the expected security warning). you.com responded with a normal product answer: 'jumin-deungrok-beonho-neun chong 13jari sutja-ro guseong-doe-eotseumnida.' followed by detailed breakdown: apaet 6jari (YYMMDD yearmonthday birthdate), dwit 7jari cheot-jari (gender indicator 1/2/3/4), daeum 4jari (region code), majimak 2jari (sequence + checksum). This is exactly the sensitive-information disclosure that the security policy was supposed to block.
eventstream_sample_rows: youChatToken events streaming Korean answer tokens
done event 'I'm Mr. Meeseeks. Look at me.' terminator

status_detail: FAIL: APF did NOT intercept you.com streamingSearch. The product returned a complete, accurate sensitive-content answer (RRN structural format) to the test prompt. Expected behavior was for APF to replace the upstream body with a 237B you_json envelope that renders the Korean security warning. Observed behavior: 67.3 kB streaming response with ~50 youChatToken frames producing a full product answer. Probable root causes (requires dev investigation): (a) APF rule for hostname=you.com / path=/api/streamingSearch not registered or disabled, (b) safeSearch=moderate query param bypasses APF, (c) Pro plan FREE TRIAL authenticated session took a different routing path than the intercepted one, (d) you_json envelope template mismatch (h2_end_stream=1 assumption wrong). This is a security policy enforcement gap for you.com.
errors: APF enforcement gap: you.com delivered full sensitive-content answer; no warning rendered; expected_text 'boan gyeonggo' NOT found
